input.headline: Josie ‘knew’ grandson Troy Parrott would score against Hungary

raw.headline: Josie ‘knew’ grandson Troy Parrott would score against Hungary

winner: Josie's psychic ability must be hungarian-ous, because she knew Troy Parrott wouldn't miss scoring against her own country!

candidates.text: Josie, if you knew Troy was going to score against Hungary, why didn't you tell him to bring a travel-sized cooler for all those goals?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the travel-sized cooler, specific reference to scoring against Hungary, and concise humor."}

candidates.text: Josie's psychic abilities must be on fire, because even she could have predicted that Troy Parrott would score against Hungary... if he was playing charades!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific, using a humorous twist on psychic abilities and charades. It's concise and surprising, though the reference to Hungary may lack context for some."}

candidates.text: Why did Josie's grandson Troy Parrott refuse to play hide and seek with his teammates against Hungary? Because he already knew he would score!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'knew' and 'score,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity about the context of the headline. The humor is mild and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: Josie's psychic ability must be hungarian-ous, because she knew Troy Parrott wouldn't miss scoring against her own country!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Hungarian' and 'hungarian-ous,' specific to the headline, concise, and surprising with wordplay."}

candidates.text: Grandma Josie confidently predicted, "I told you so, now who's the scoring grandma?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the twist of 'scoring grandma,' but it lacks specificity about Josie's prediction and feels a bit forced in context."}
